name: Ujjayanta Palace
description: A magnificent royal house standing gracefully in the heart of Agartala, boasting pristine white architecture and sprawling Mughal-style gardens. It serves as a striking window into the grandeur of the Manikya dynasty.
highlights: Surrounded by two picturesque artificial lakes
Houses the State Museum detailing Northeast heritage
howToReach: Centrally located in Agartala, easily accessible by auto-rickshaws or walking from major city points.
tip: Visit during the evening when the palace is beautifully illuminated against the night sky.

name: Heritage Park
description: A sprawling green oasis that masterfully encapsulates the cultural and historical essence of the state in a miniature format. It offers a serene escape with dedicated zones showcasing indigenous tribal life.
highlights: Miniature replicas of famous state monuments
Lush botanical spaces with native flora
howToReach: Situated near Kunjaban, about 3 km from Agartala city center, reachable by local cabs.
tip: Carry a camera for the miniature monument section, as it perfectly captures the state's essence.

name: Jagannath Temple
description: An architectural marvel adorned with intricate carvings and vibrant orange hues, deeply rooted in Vaishnava traditions. This spiritual sanctuary offers a peaceful retreat right near the royal palace grounds.
highlights: Peaceful pond adjacent to the main shrine
bestTime: Any time of the year; July for the Rath Yatra festival
howToReach: Located just meters away from Ujjayanta Palace gate in Agartala.
tip: Respect the temple customs by dressing modestly and enjoying the quiet ambiance.

name: Chaturdash Devta Temple
description: A revered ancient shrine dedicated to fourteen deities, representing a unique blend of tribal animism and traditional Hindu practices. The distinctive roof style mirrors traditional Bengali hut architecture.
highlights: Heads of fourteen gods worshipped simultaneously
Host to the massive annual Kharchi Puja
bestTime: July during the Kharchi festival, or winter months
howToReach: Around 8 km from Agartala, best reached by hiring a private taxi or auto.
tip: If visiting during Kharchi Puja, prepare for massive crowds and vibrant local fairs.

name: Laxmi Narayan Temple
description: A prominent spiritual center located within the palace compound, known for its elegant white facade and tranquil environment. It has been a cornerstone of local devotion for over a century.
howToReach: Situated next to the Ujjayanta Palace main entrance in Agartala.
tip: Attend the evening aarti (prayer) for a spiritually uplifting experience.

name: Uma Maheswar Temple
description: Nestled amidst lush greenery near the palace lakes, this saffron-hued shrine is dedicated to Lord Shiva and Parvati. Its riverside location provides a cooling breeze and scenic views.
highlights: Scenic location overlooking the palace lake
Serene environment away from city noise
howToReach: A short walk from the Ujjayanta Palace grounds.
tip: Combine this visit with a leisurely walk around the adjoining lakes.

name: Benuban Vihar
description: A peaceful Buddhist shrine tucked into the Kunjaban area, shaded by thick groves of trees. It holds beautiful metal idols crafted in Myanmar, reflecting trans-border spiritual ties.
bestTime: October to April; May for Buddha Purnima
howToReach: Located 2 km from the main city, accessible via cycle rickshaws.
tip: Maintain silence as monks frequently meditate in the open courtyard.

name: Gedu Mian Mosque
description: A stunning piece of Islamic architecture featuring multiple white domes and slender minarets, built by a prominent local merchant. Intricate marble inlays adorn its interior halls.
highlights: Elegant white marble and tile work
Historically significant to the local Muslim community
howToReach: Located in the Shibnagar area of Agartala.
tip: Avoid visiting during Friday afternoon prayers unless participating.

name: Rabindra Kanan
description: A lush, well-maintained park adjacent to the Raj Bhavan, historically associated with Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore. Thick foliage and quiet pathways make it a favored spot for peaceful strolls.
highlights: Rich diversity of local trees and flowers
howToReach: Next to the Raj Bhavan (Governor's House) in Agartala.
tip: Perfect for a morning walk or reading a book under the shade.

name: State Museum
description: Operating out of the monumental Ujjayanta Palace, this repository guards the rich cultural, archaeological, and tribal history of Northeast India. It showcases rare stone sculptures and historic coins.
highlights: Detailed exhibits on indigenous tribal cultures
howToReach: Housed inside Ujjayanta Palace, Agartala city center.
tip: Allocate at least two hours to properly view all the expansive galleries.

name: Portuguese Church
description: A modest yet historically profound structure at Mariamnagar that traces the legacy of Portuguese mercenaries employed by the Manikya kings. It paints a picture of early European presence in the region.
howToReach: Mariamnagar is roughly 5 km from central Agartala.
tip: Engage with the locals to hear fascinating stories of the Portuguese settlers.

name: Baramura Hills
description: A verdant hill range offering winding roads, deep green valleys, and mist-covered mornings. It is an adventurous corridor connecting the capital to the state's interior.
howToReach: About 35 km from Agartala, easily accessible via the National Highway.
tip: Drive carefully as the winding uphill roads can get foggy early in the day.

name: Baramura Eco Park
description: Nestled in the lush folds of the Baramura range, this eco-park is designed to harmonize with nature rather than disrupt it. It features rustic wooden bridges over gently flowing streams.
highlights: Rustic wooden bridges and tribal huts
howToReach: Located along the highway, 40 km from Agartala.
tip: Bring your own food and water as internal kiosk options are limited.

name: Baramura Waterfall
description: A hidden cascade tucked deep within the tropical forests of the Baramura range. The soothing sound of falling water echoes through the untouched wilderness.
howToReach: Requires a short hike from the main Baramura Eco Park area.
tip: Wear sturdy trekking shoes as the trail can be slippery.

name: Botanical Garden
description: A dedicated space for the conservation of the region's rare flora, offering meticulously curated plant collections. The peaceful environment is perfect for botanical enthusiasts and casual walkers alike.
howToReach: Easily accessible from the main Agartala city areas by auto.
tip: Look out for the medicinal plant section which highlights traditional remedies.

name: Children’s Park
description: A vibrant and bustling recreational ground in the city center, offering open play areas and colorful play equipment. It frequently host local fairs and cultural events.
howToReach: Located centrally in Agartala.
tip: Great place to catch local handicraft exhibitions during the winter season.

name: College Tilla Viewpoint
description: Located near the historic MBB College, this elevated viewpoint overlooks a large tranquil lake. The colonial architecture of the college adds a nostalgic charm to the panoramic views.
highlights: Views of the expansive college lake
Popular local hangout for sunset views
howToReach: Located within the MBB College campus, Agartala.
tip: Visit during late afternoon to catch a stunning reflection of the sunset on the lake.

name: Neermahal
description: A breathtaking architectural marvel seemingly floating on the waters of Rudrasagar Lake. This former royal summer residence blends Hindu and Mughal styles perfectly against a watery horizon.
highlights: Boat rides across the scenic lake
howToReach: Located in Melaghar, about 53 km from Agartala. Best reached by taxi followed by a boat ride.
tip: Take the traditional wooden boat rather than a motorboat for a more romantic experience.

name: Rudrasagar Lake
description: An expansive wetland that provides a mirror-like setting for Neermahal and sustains diverse aquatic life. It transforms into a birdwatcher's paradise during the migratory season.
bestTime: November to February for bird watching
howToReach: Melaghar is the base, 53 km from Agartala via road.
tip: Rent a local boat early in the morning to spot rare migratory birds without the crowds.

name: Sepahijala Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A dense woodland sanctuary famous for harboring the rare bespectacled Phayre’s Leaf Monkey. The region blends zoological gardens, botanical sections, and open lakes into one vast natural reserve.
highlights: Home to the Phayre’s Leaf Monkey
Boating facilities on the forest lake
howToReach: Roughly 25 km from Agartala, easily accessible by bus or taxi.
tip: Opt for the silent battery-operated vehicles to explore the vast sanctuary without tiring.

name: Tepania Eco Park
description: A highly popular outdoor retreat featuring unique treehouses and orchid enclosures. It provides an immersive forest experience wrapped in comfortable, family-friendly infrastructure.
highlights: Stay options in unique wooden treehouses
howToReach: Close to Udaipur-Agartala highway, around 45 km from the capital.
tip: Book the treehouse accommodations well in advance if planning an overnight stay.

name: Sepahijala Botanical Garden
description: Situated within the larger sanctuary complex, this segment is dedicated to the preservation of rare orchids and regional timbers. It serves as a living laboratory for the state's diverse plant life.
howToReach: Part of the Sepahijala Wildlife Sanctuary complex.
tip: Ask for a local forest guide to help identify the subtle and rare blooming orchids.

name: Mini Zoo Complex
description: A specialized conservation breeding center located within Sepahijala that focuses on protecting indigenous wildlife. It offers visitors close-up views of the region's top predators and primates.
howToReach: Inside the Sepahijala sanctuary, 25 km from Agartala.
tip: Visit early in the morning when the animals are most active and visible.

name: Eco Trails (Melaghar Belt)
description: Untouched forest trails meandering through the region surrounding Melaghar. These paths expose the raw agricultural rural life intermixed with vibrant tropical woodland.
howToReach: Trails start from various points around Melaghar town.
tip: Carry insect repellent and wear light cotton clothes for a comfortable trek.

name: Tripura Sundari Temple
description: Regarded as one of the 51 Shakti Peethas, this ancient, tortoise-shaped shrine radiates intense spiritual energy. Thousands flock here seeking blessings, bathing the site in an aura of deep devotion.
bestTime: Any time; Diwali for the massive festival
howToReach: Located in Udaipur, about 55 km from Agartala.
tip: Buy the local 'Peda' sweet from the vibrant stalls outside as a sacred offering.

name: Bhubaneswari Temple
description: Perched gracefully on the high banks of the Gomati River, this historic temple was immortalized in the literature of Rabindranath Tagore. It offers a nostalgic glimpse into the eras of past kings.
howToReach: Udaipur town, adjacent to the old palace ruins.
tip: Read Tagore’s play 'Bisarjan' before visiting to appreciate the site's literary significance.

name: Gunabati Group of Temples
description: A mysterious and beautiful cluster of three brick temples standing together in harmony. While their exact origins are debated, their structural elegance from the 17th century is unquestionable.
howToReach: Situated in Udaipur, close to the main market area.
tip: Examine the eroded terracotta plaques on the walls to uncover antique floral motifs.

name: Kalyan Sagar Lake
description: A massive, serene water body lying adjacent to the Tripura Sundari Temple. Its sacred waters are home to large schools of fish and rare species of soft-shell turtles revered by pilgrims.
highlights: Spiritual atmosphere right by the temple
howToReach: Right next to the Tripura Sundari Temple in Udaipur.
tip: Purchase puffed rice from nearby vendors to gently feed the sacred turtles.

name: Bijoy Sagar Lake
description: Also known as Mahadev Dighi, this large lake is bordered by historic shrines and lush landscapes. It stands as a testament to the water management skills of the ancient Manikya rulers.
howToReach: Located centrally in Udaipur town.
tip: Enjoy a quiet evening stroll along the paved borders as the sunsets over the water.

name: Amar Sagar Lake
description: An artificially excavated lake of grand proportions, stretching out beautifully down from Amarpur. Its banks provide deeply shaded spots perfect for picnics and leisurely afternoons.
highlights: Vast clear waters bordered by trees
howToReach: Located in Amarpur, about 25 km from Udaipur.
tip: Pack a lovely picnic basket as food options around the lake are sparse.

name: Jagannath Dighi
description: One of Udaipur's many ancient lakes, known for its pristine waters and tranquil surroundings. It reflects the sky beautifully, bordered by old settlements and temples.
howToReach: Central Udaipur, easily accessible by auto-rickshaw.
tip: A good spot for photography enthusiasts looking for calm water reflections.

name: Gomati River
description: The lifeline of the district, this gentle river winds through valleys and plains, sustaining agriculture and wildlife. The riverbanks are dotted with ancient ruins and rolling green hills.
howToReach: Flows through Udaipur and Amarpur.
tip: Catch a local ferry crossing for an authentic experience of local river life.

name: Chabimura (Devtamura)
description: Often called the 'Amazon of India', this steep, densely forested gorge features colossal rock carvings of deities on sheer cliffs. The boat ride through the narrow, misty river is purely awe-inspiring.
howToReach: Drive to Amarpur (roughly 80 km from Agartala), then hire a motorboat.
tip: Book your boat ride in advance during peak tourist season as availability is limited.

name: Chabimura Waterfall
description: A pristine waterfall hidden within the dense foliage of the Devtamura hills, accessible only via a rugged trek or boat. The water crashes down into a secluded pristine pool.
howToReach: Accessible during the Chabimura boat tour via a short jungle trek.
tip: Wear footwear with excellent grip, as the mossy rocks near the fall are very slippery.

name: Dumboor Lake
description: An incredibly vast, drum-shaped reservoir dotted with 48 mesmerizing lush green islands. The sheer expanse of the blue water against the hill backdrop feels almost oceanic.
howToReach: Roughly 110 km from Agartala, travel via Amarpur.
tip: Take a boat to 'Narkel Kunja' island located within the lake for a tropical retreat.

name: Dumboor Waterfall
description: Locally known as the source of the Gomati River, this powerful cascade offers a spectacular sight. The rushing waters cut through rocky terrains, creating a loud, refreshing ambiance.
highlights: The dramatic origin point of Gomati river
howToReach: Near Dumboor Lake, accessible by a slightly rough terrain road.
tip: Keep electronics heavily waterproofed due to the heavy mist generated by the falls.

name: Rajarshi Memorial Park
description: A beautifully landscaped tribute park situated in Udaipur, dedicated to the legacy of the Manikya kings. It offers perfectly manicured lawns and serene pathways overlooking historical ruins.
howToReach: Located near the Bhubaneswari Temple in Udaipur.
tip: An excellent safe space for kids to run around securely while you relax.

name: Longtharai Hills
description: A sprawling, mystical mountain range deeply embedded in regional folklore. Its deep valleys and dense bamboo forests house secluded tribal hamlets living in harmony with nature.
howToReach: Accessible via the national highway heading towards Ambassa.
tip: Stop at the high-altitude bypasses to capture stunning panoramic valley photos.

name: Longtharai Mandir
description: A highly revered spiritual site dedicated to Lord Shiva, located deep within the Longtharai hills. It serves as an important pilgrimage site for the indigenous populations of the district.
bestTime: Occurs during local Shivratri festivals, or winter
howToReach: Off the main highway in Dhalai, requires a short uphill drive.
tip: Respect the local tribal customs which often blend seamlessly with Hindu worship here.

name: Kamaleswari Temple
description: A beautifully maintained temple in Kamalpur, dedicated to Goddess Kali. It stands near the international border and holds immense emotional significance for the local residents.
howToReach: Situated in the border town of Kamalpur.
tip: Visit during the evening arti to experience the strong communal harmony of the border town.

name: Saika Waterfall
description: A sparkling, relatively unexplored cascading waterfall hidden within the rugged terrains of Dhalai. The trek to reach it is as thrilling as the refreshing plunge pool at its base.
howToReach: Requires local guidance from Ambassa or nearby villages.
tip: Hire a local villager as a guide since the forest paths are unmarked.

name: Tuisoi Waterfall
description: A picturesque rocky cascade offering absolute solitude amidst nature. The rhythmic sound of water hitting the large boulders provides a naturally therapeutic environment.
howToReach: Located deep in Dhalai district; transport via private vehicle followed by hiking.
tip: Carry a full first-aid kit as there are no immediate medical facilities nearby.

name: Sanaiya Waterfall
description: A charming, multi-tiered waterfall flanked by dense tropical greenery. Known for its mesmerizing flow post-monsoon, it’s a favorite hidden picnic spot for locals.
howToReach: Best reached by hiring an SUV from Ambassa.
tip: Leave no trace; carry back all your plastic waste to keep the hidden falls pristine.

name: Manu River Valley
description: A breathtakingly lush valley shaped by the meandering Manu River. The valley is characterized by fertile floodplains, dense forests, and vibrant hillside tribal settlements.
howToReach: Drive along the highway passing through Manu town.
tip: Pull over at riverside roadside stalls to try fresh, locally grown bamboo shoot delicacies.

name: Narkel Kunja Island
description: A breathtaking island paradise located on Dumboor Lake, dotted entirely with coconut trees. It provides a Maldives-like vibe surrounded by fresh hill waters and clear skies.
highlights: Hundreds of scenic coconut palm trees
howToReach: Bordering Dhalai/Gomati, reachable via a motorboat ride from the mainland.
tip: Spend a night in the newly built log huts for an unforgettable starry night experience.

name: Forest Trek Routes (Longtharai)
description: A network of rugged, adventurous trekking paths crisscrossing the Longtharai hills. These raw trails offer trekkers an authentic experience of Tripura's dense, untamed jungles.
howToReach: Trails usually commence from the foothill villages along the highway.
tip: Wear full-sleeved clothing to protect against jungle insects and thorny underbrush.

name: Remote Hill Streams
description: Crystal-clear seasonal streams cutting through the rocky terrains of Dhalai. These quiet waterways offer perfect spots to rest and cool down during summer hikes.
howToReach: Scattered across the district, accessible via hiking.
tip: Sit quietly by the banks; you might spot unique amphibious wildlife characteristic of the area.

name: Khowai Valley
description: An enchanting, fertile stretch of flatland hugged by gentle hills and split by the Khowai River. The valley is known for its peaceful rural charm and vibrant agricultural fields.
howToReach: A direct drive of about 50 km from Agartala.
tip: Visit during the harvest season to fully appreciate the golden hues of the valley.

name: Montang Valley
description: A relatively new, wildly popular high-altitude destination offering sweeping, clouds-below-your-feet views. Sitting atop the Atharamura range, it feels like a piece of heaven on earth.
bestTime: Monsoon and early winter (August to December)
howToReach: Requires a steep uphill drive off the national highway via Teliamura.
tip: Try the traditional bamboo-cooked rice and pork from the local stalls perched on the cliff.

name: Baramura Range
description: The extension of the grand Baramura hills into Khowai, offering some of the most scenic highway drives in Northeast India. The rich tropical forests here are incredibly dense.
bestTime: Year-round, taking caution during heavy rains
howToReach: Forms the dividing highway path between West Tripura and Khowai.
tip: Keep your windows rolled down to enjoy the incredibly fresh, pine-scented air.

name: Dumula Waterfall
description: A gorgeous, secluded waterfall crashing down rocky outcrops into a calm pool. It serves as a pristine natural retreat far from commercial tourism.
highlights: Great spot for an outdoor picnic
howToReach: Requires a sturdy vehicle and local directions from Khowai town.
tip: Carry a change of clothes if you plan to wade into the inviting plunge pool.

name: Forest Ridge Viewpoints
description: Scattered high-altitude clearings that look over the vast Khowai and Atharamura valleys. They provide unparalleled opportunities for landscape photography, especially at sunrise.
howToReach: Located along the higher elevations of the Tripura highways.
tip: Arrive just before dawn to watch the mist slowly lift off the dense forest canopy.

name: Riverbank Picnic Spots
description: Sandy, open stretches along the meandering Khowai River. During winter weekends, these spots become lively social hubs for locals enjoying outdoor feasts.
howToReach: Accessible from various points within Khowai town constraints.
tip: Blend in by packing a traditional Bengali-style picnic lunch with rice and fish curry.

name: Bamboo Forest Trails
description: Extensive, naturally formed walkways passing through towering, thick bamboo groves. The sunlight filtering through the green bamboo leaves creates a magical, serene environment.
highlights: Eerie, beautiful sound of wind through bamboo
howToReach: Found in the rural outskirts of Khowai district.
tip: Walk quietly; the acoustics of the bamboo forest amplify the rustling wind beautifully.

name: Jampui Hills
description: The highest hill range in the state, rightfully dubbed the 'Eternal Hills of Spring'. It boasts a pleasant climate year-round, stunning sunrise views, and vibrant Mizo culture.
howToReach: About 200 km from Agartala, requiring a drive to Kanchanpur and then uphill.
tip: Don't miss the spectacular sunset over the neighboring hills of Mizoram.

name: Vanghmun Village
description: A pristine, impeccably clean village situated atop the Jampui hills. It serves as the cultural heart of the hills, renowned for its beautifully maintained gardens and warm hospitality.
howToReach: The primary settlement hub of the Jampui Hills range.
tip: Interact with the friendly locals; they often invite visitors in for a cup of warm tea.

name: Betlingchhip Peak
description: Standing at 930 meters, this is the highest geographical point in Tripura. From the watchtower here, one can see sweeping views encompassing parts of Mizoram and Bangladesh.
bestTime: October to February for clear skies
howToReach: A short hike or drive from Vanghmun village.
tip: Carry binoculars to clearly spot landmarks across the Bangladesh border.

name: Shilchhara Buddha Waterfall
description: A unique waterfall site hidden deeply in the northern jungles, near which an ancient rock carving of Lord Buddha exists. The blend of raw nature and ancient history is profound.
howToReach: Deep in North Tripura; requires specific local guidance and trekking.
tip: Visit with a group; the area is extremely remote with no mobile network connectivity.

name: Thaidawr Ridge
description: A breathtaking high-altitude ridge walk offering uninterrupted views of clouds rolling over deep green valleys. It feels completely disconnected from the bustling world below.
howToReach: Part of the greater Jampui Hills trekking routes.
tip: Ensure you carry a windcheater, as the ridge winds can be surprisingly biting.

name: Orange Garden Trails
description: During winter, these hillsides transform into a sea of orange. Walking through the laden orchards of Jampui is a sensory delight, filled with sweet citrus aromas.
highlights: Opportunities to buy fresh fruit directly
howToReach: Spread across Vanghmun and other Jampui villages.
tip: Always ask permission before plucking an orange straight from a farmer's tree.

name: Sunrise Point (Jampui)
description: A designated edge on the Jampui hills that offers arguably the most spectacular dawn in the state. Watching the sun break through the thick valley mist is unforgettable.
howToReach: Easily accessible via an early morning walk from Vanghmun.
tip: Wake up at least 45 minutes before local sunrise to catch the full color transition.

name: Village Homestay Circuits
description: An organized network of warm, welcoming homestays run by the Mizo families of Jampui. It provides the ultimate authentic experience of high-altitude tribal life and cuisine.
howToReach: Available throughout the villages in the Jampui range.
tip: Try the traditional smoked pork and bamboo shoot preparations offered by your hosts.

name: Unakoti Rock Carvings
description: A colossal, mind-boggling open-air art gallery nestled in forested hills, featuring massive 7th-century rock-cut sculptures of Hindu deities. The centerpiece is the 30-foot tall face of Lord Shiva.
highlights: Gigantic rock-carved Shiva head (Unakotiswara Kal Bhairava)
howToReach: About 8 km from Kailashahar; easily reached by auto or taxi.
tip: Prepare for a lot of steep stair climbing; wear the most comfortable walking shoes you own.

name: Khowrabil Lake
description: A large, tranquil water body situated near Kailashahar. It provides a peaceful respite with reflections of the sky and distant hills making it a picturesque resting spot.
highlights: Good spot for witnessing local fishing
howToReach: Located near the outskirts of Kailashahar town.
tip: Visit during late afternoon to sketch or photograph the quiet village life along the banks.

name: Maa Bhabatarini Temple
description: A highly revered Hindu shrine in Kailashahar, deeply respected by the local populace. It acts as the spiritual heart of the town, bustling with devotion and chanting during evenings.
highlights: Central spiritual hub of the district
howToReach: Centrally located in Kailashahar town.
tip: Maintain the sanctity of the compound by adopting silent observation during prayers.

name: Unakoti Hills
description: Beyond the carvings, the rugged Raghunandan hills housing Unakoti are spectacular in their own right. They are draped in deep greenery, featuring hidden streams and diverse avian life.
howToReach: Encompasses the area right around the Unakoti heritage site.
tip: Look closely into the foliage; the hills are home to unique regional bird species.

name: Forest Trails (Unakoti)
description: Steep, narrow paths cutting through the forested area around the Unakoti heritage site. These shaded trails offer a sense of exploration akin to discovering a lost ancient civilization.
howToReach: Branching off from the main Unakoti rock carving complex.
tip: Stay on the visibly trodden paths as the deeper jungle is thick and disorienting.

name: Hill Streams
description: Delightful mini-waterfalls and rivulets that crisscross the stone carvings of Unakoti. During the monsoon, they swell and cascade beautifully directly over the ancient rock faces.
bestTime: August to October (for peak water flow)
howToReach: Interweaved within the Unakoti rock carving stairs.
tip: Be extremely cautious on the main rock stairs during monsoon, as the stream water makes them slick.

name: Pilak Archaeological Site
description: A treasure trove of antiquities dating back to the 8th century, revealing an incredible peaceful coexistence of Hindu and Buddhist cultures. Unearthed terracotta plaques and stone images dot this lush landscape.
howToReach: Located near Jolaibari, roughly 100 km from Agartala.
tip: The site is spread out across agricultural fields; hire a local auto to comfortably navigate between excavation pits.

name: Feni River
description: A historically and geographically significant river that naturally borders India and Bangladesh. The riverbanks offer stunning sunset views and a tangible connection to the neighboring nation.
howToReach: Flows swiftly past Sabroom town.
tip: Take a moment to admire the modern Maitri Setu bridge, symbolizing cross-border connectivity.

name: Maitree Udyan
description: A meticulously maintained modern park built to commemorate the friendship between India and Bangladesh. It features wide walkways, historical monuments, and blooming flower gardens overlooking the border.
howToReach: Situated in Chottakhola, accessible by road from Belonia.
tip: Spend time reading the plaques to deeply understand the region's role in the 1971 liberation war.

name: Bardosh Eco Park
description: A quiet, green lung in the district providing a perfect escape into nature for local families. The park is characterized by dense tree cover, small streams, and rustic seating arrangements.
howToReach: Located easily accessible off the main district roads.
tip: A great spot to string up a hammock between trees and read a book in total peace.

name: Dhananjoy Tripura Eco Park
description: A beautifully designed eco-centric recreational area promoting environmental awareness. It seamlessly blends manicured lawns with the wilder, natural flora of the region.
howToReach: Positioned within the South Tripura district boundaries.
tip: Perfect place for an educational botanical walk with children.

name: Hrishyamukh Hills
description: Gentle, rolling hills offering a stark contrast to the dramatic peaks of the north. The area is dotted with small, vibrant villages and offers immense peace away from any urban chaos.
howToReach: Accessible via scenic rural roads from Belonia.
tip: Hire a bicycle or motorbike to truly enjoy the winding, gentle roads of this area.

name: Chottakhola Border Viewpoint
description: A strategically located viewpoint historically tied to the Bangladesh Liberation War. Today, it offers sweeping, unimpeded views directly into the plains of the neighboring country.
howToReach: Located in Chottakhola, near the Maitree Udyan.
tip: Bring a camera with a good zoom lens to capture the fascinating cross-border landscapes.
